<!-- generated by specd -- do not edit -->

# Render spec templates

Renders a jinja template into specs.

# CLI

- `render` is a valid positional argument to the `specd` command line entrypoint
- When the user calls `render` all templates under the specd `templates` directory are rendered into the specd `specs` directory
- When the user calls `render`, each template that is rendered to a spec file is represented in the terminal as `  <scope>.specd.md -> <specs directory name>/<scope>.md`

# Template discovery

- a file is only recognised as a spec template if it is named in the form *.specd.md
- If the specd `templates` directory is configured with a pyproject.toml, that is the directory in which templates are discovered
- if `render` is called with the `-t` or `--templates` options, templates are discovered only at the supplied location
- If the specd `templates` directory is not configured with a pyproject.toml and the `-t` or `--templates` options are not used, the templates are discovered in `templates/` under the cwd
- If no templates are found in the specd `templates` directory, `render` prints `No *.specd.md templates found in <templates dir name>/`
- If no directory exists at the specd `templates` directory location, a warning is printed: "The expected templates dir does not exist"; then it exits code 1

# Spec rendering target

- If the specd `specs` directory is configured with a pyproject.toml, that is the directory in which specs are written
- If `render` is called with the `-s` or `--specs` options, specs are written into the supplied location
- If the specd `specs` directory is not configured with a pyproject.toml and the `-s` or `--specs` options are not used, the specs are written into `specs/` under the cwd
- If no directory exists at the specd `specs` directory location, a warning is printed: "The expected specs dir does not exist"; then it exits code 1

# Spec rendering behaviour

- A template named in the form <scope>.specd.md is rendered into the specs directory with the name <scope>.md.
- Every output file starts with `<!-- generated by specd -- do not edit -->\\n\\n`
- All jinja syntax in the template is evaluated in the rendered spec
- A template with no jinja syntax is rendered into a spec with identical content + the "generated by specd" comment
- When Jinja2 errors arise in rendering they are allowed to propagate and print to stderr, then it exits code 1

## --help

- Calling `render` with `--help` or `-h` prints help
- Help text includes `usage: specd render [-h] [-t TEMPLATES] [-s SPECS] [-w]\\n\\n`
- Help text includes `options:\\n`
- Help text includes `  -h, --help            show this help message and exit\\n`
- Help text includes `  -t, --templates TEMPLATES\\n` + `Path to the templates directory` 
- Help text includes `  -s, --specs SPECS     Path to the specs output directory`
- Help text includes `  -w, --watch           Watch templates for changes and re-render automatically`
- Help text is followed by a blank line

# --watch

- Calling `render` with `--watch` or `-w` occupies the terminal with a blocking file watcher that runs until the process is interrupted
- When called with `--watch` or `-w`, the saving of any template triggers template rendering as though `render` were called
- When called with `--watch` or `-w`, non-save-related events on a template do not trigger template rendering
- When called with `--watch` or `-w`, and when rendering is triggered, the normal `render` output is preceded by `Change detected: <path to changed file from templates dir>\\n`
